commit.message:
Pin .NET SDK to 10.0.1xx band (10.0.105) to fix CI (#3582)

* Pin .NET SDK to 10.0.1xx band (10.0.105) to fix CI

The CI agents have .NET SDK 10.0.201 installed, which requires MSBuild
18.0+ that isn't available in VS 2022. The global.json rollForward
policy 'latestFeature' was allowing the SDK to roll from 10.0.1xx to
10.0.2xx, breaking the WinUI native build.

Changes:
- global.json: pin to 10.0.105, rollForward latestFeature -> latestPatch
- azure-templates-variables.yml: DOTNET_VERSION and DOTNET_WORKLOAD_VERSION -> 10.0.105

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Pin .NET SDK to 10.0.1xx feature band

- global.json: lower version floor to 10.0.100 so pre-installed 1xx SDKs are accepted
- Docker (debian/10, debian/13, alpine): change dotnet-install.sh channel from 10.0 to 10.0.1xx
- Docker (wasm): switch from mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:10.0 base image to debian:12 + dotnet-install.sh with 10.0.1xx channel

The 10.0.2xx feature band has issues on Windows. This ensures all
CI builds use the 1xx feature band consistently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: agenda system, bug fix (#123)

* feat: topic agenda system with concept-based onboarding

Replace flat skill selection with a structured topic agenda that
supports role-based concept browsing, prerequisite-aware ordering,
and configurable assessment thoroughness (quick/standard/thorough).

Backend:
- Add AgendaItem, TopicStatus, Thoroughness state types
- Add build_agenda pipeline node between calibration and assessment
- Add GET /roles/{role_id}/concepts endpoint with topo-sorted concepts
- Add display_name field to all knowledge base YAML concepts
- Infer confidence for un-assessed concepts from assessed prerequisites
- Dynamic question budget based on agenda size and thoroughness
- Conclude assessment when no pending topics remain

Frontend:
- Replace SkillBrowser with ConceptBrowser grouped by level
- Fetch concepts per role/level via new API endpoint
- Add max 10 concept selection limit with disabled state
- Show estimated question count from thoroughness setting

* fix: update assessment route test mocks for concept-based target graph

When roleId is provided, the route now calls get_target_graph_for_concepts
instead of get_target_graph. Tests without roleId also need get_all_topics
mocked for the new estimated_questions computation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: auto-create openlearning_test DB on postgres container init

Mount an init script in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/ so the test
database is created alongside the main database on first container
startup. Prevents local test failures after volume recreation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review — active topic conclude, propagation overwrite, agenda fallback

1. decide_branch: include TopicStatus.active in remaining check so the
   deeper/probe loop runs up to max_questions_per_topic before concluding
2. _propagate_prereq_confidence: never overwrite existing KG nodes —
   only create new nodes for unassessed prerequisites
3. build_agenda_node: remove silent fallback to full agenda when
   skill_ids produce no matches, keeping agenda consistent with
   the filtered target_graph

commit.message:
feat: DevTunnel, snapfeed feedback, bandwidth stats, Gastown, Go TUI

## DevTunnel support
- Named persistent tunnels (stable URL across restarts)
- Auto-detection: prefers devtunnel, falls back to cloudflare
- CLI: --tunnel-provider auto|devtunnel|cloudflare

## Snapfeed UI feedback (@microsoft/snapfeed)
- GitHub Device Flow auth (user's own identity)
- Server-side token storage (~/.remux/github-token)
- GitHub OAuth proxy endpoints (CORS bypass)
- Telemetry endpoint: POST /api/telemetry/events (SQLite)
- Issues include: message, context, console errors, network log,
  session replay, screenshot (opt-in)

## Server extensions
- Terminal state tracker (@xterm/headless, mosh-like diffs)
- Bandwidth stats (rolling window, RTT, diff tracking)
- Push notifications (VAPID, bell/exit detection)
- Gastown workspace detection + session enrichment
- Structured event watcher (events.jsonl)
- File browser API (GET /api/files)

## Go TUI client (tui/)
- Multi-host terminal client (bubbletea + lipgloss)
- Client-side scrollback cache
- 6 unit + 2 integration tests

## start.ps1
- Stable token across restarts (~/.remux/token)
- --prod / dev mode with proper flag forwarding

fix(case-service): add missing CaseAccessChecker mock and clear security context

CaseServiceImpl acquired a CaseAccessChecker dependency but
CaseServiceImplTest was never updated to include a matching @Mock.
When another test in the CI run left a JwtAuthenticationToken in
SecurityContextHolder, tryGetAuthContext() returned non-null and the
null caseAccessChecker caused NullPointerException in testGetCaseById.

Changes:
- Add @Mock CaseAccessChecker caseAccessChecker so @InjectMocks can
  wire it correctly (prevents NPE regardless of security context state)
- Add @BeforeEach SecurityContextHolder.clearContext() to prevent
  security context leakage from other tests contaminating this suite

Fixes recurring CI failure: CaseServiceImplTest.testGetCaseById NPE.

fix(soap-audit): mask PII at point of capture in ClsSoapAuditInterceptor

Raw SOAP bodies containing CPR numbers, names and account numbers were
stored in MessageContext as 'rawRequestBody'/'rawResponseBody' and only
masked in afterCompletion(). This left unmasked PII in memory for the
full request lifecycle.

Change: mask immediately in handleRequest/handleResponse/handleFault.
Raw body is a local variable only (needed for service/operation resolution
and fault extraction). MessageContext now stores pre-masked values.
afterCompletion() reads 'maskedRequestBody'/'maskedResponseBody' directly.

No change to CLS audit event content. 59/59 tests pass. Closes TB-031.

commit.message:
fix: edit modal save closes window and shows save feedback (#20)

The save button in the edit modal had no error boundary — if
`updateProduct` or `renderProductList` threw (e.g. localStorage quota
exceeded), `cleanup()` was never reached and the modal stayed open.
There was also no confirmation that the save succeeded.

## Changes

- **`showToast(message, type)`** — new helper that appends a temporary
`role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"` div at the top of the screen,
fades out after 3 s
- **`onSave` wrapped in try/catch:**
- Success → `cleanup()` closes modal, then green `✅ Produkt gespeichert`
toast
- Error → inline `edit-error-msg` shows `"Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte
versuche es erneut."`, modal stays open for retry

```ts
try {
  updateProduct(product.id, { name, expiryDate, notifyDaysBefore });
  renderProductList(container);
  checkAndNotify();
  cleanup();
  showToast('✅ Produkt gespeichert');
} catch {
  if (errorMsg) errorMsg.textContent = 'Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte versuche es erneut.';
}
```

## Screenshot

![Modal open before
save](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14683926-a0ae-4c6a-bfa2-729bd1a79b5d)
![Modal closed with success
toast](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7f085375-e2f1-4f06-bef8-ce18b86dd2e1)

feat: add Windows Installer, Windows Update error sources

Add MSI return codes (3010, 1603, etc.) and Windows Update error codes
(WU_E_*) as new products. Improve Exchange and Intune error parsing.

- New repo: MicrosoftDocs/win32 for MSI error codes (1127 entries)
- URL-based scrape of learn.microsoft.com for WU error codes (516 entries)
- Improved Intune parser handles hex/dec dual-code columns
- Extended Exchange sparse paths (support/exchange, support/exchange-online)
- Updated _CODE_PATTERNS regex to accept short numeric codes (0-99999)
- Both numeric and symbolic MSI codes indexed for search
- Added GitHub Pages link to README

commit.message:
redesign: anti-AI aesthetic — warm editorial logbook UI

Complete frontend rewrite with intentionally non-AI design choices:
- Light-first (warm cream #f8f5f0), not dark-first
- Serif display font (Fraunces) + humanist sans (Karla), not Inter/Outfit
- Zero rounded corners — sharp edges throughout
- Warm earthy palette (brick red, stone, olive), not blue/purple
- Dense table-row layout, not spacious card grid
- Paper grain texture overlay
- Product tabs instead of colored pills
- Dashed rule separators, not drop shadows
- Single accent color (vermillion), not rainbow product colors
- Print-friendly, information-dense reference aesthetic

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
